incoming_email:
Subject: Urgent: Incorrect Billing Charge on Invoice INV-88901

Hi Hiver Support Team,

I am writing to dispute our latest invoice (INV-88901) for this month. We were billed $150.00, which is incorrect. We are on the Growth tier, which is supposed to be a flat rate of $50.00 per month. There is a line item for a 'contact overage fee' of $100.00, but our active contacts list is well within the Growth tier limit of 5,000 contacts. We currently only have 4,200 active contacts. Could you please audit this billing error and issue a refund or correction as soon as possible? This is a critical discrepancy that our accounting department needs resolved immediately.

Best regards,
Sarah Jenkins
Operations Manager, CloudSync Inc.
historical_response:
Subject: Re: Urgent: Incorrect Billing Charge on Invoice INV-88901

Dear Sarah,

Thank you for reaching out to Hiver Support, and I apologize for the confusion and concern caused by this billing discrepancy. I understand how important it is to keep your accounts accurate, especially for your accounting team.

I have investigated invoice INV-88901 and confirmed that a system error triggered an incorrect contact overage charge of $100.00, even though your active contacts (4,200) are indeed below the 5,000 limit for the Growth tier.

To resolve this, I have processed a one-time credit of $100.00 to your account. This credit will automatically be applied to your next invoice. Please note that the processing of this credit adjustment will take approximately 3 to 5 business days to reflect in your billing portal. We have also resolved the underlying system glitch to prevent this from happening in the future.

If you have any further questions or if your accounting department requires an updated copy of the current invoice, please let me know, and I will be happy to assist.

Best regards,
Alex Rivera
Customer Success Team, Hiver
ground_truth_resolution_points: Acknowledge that the customer was incorrectly billed $150 instead of the $50 Growth tier flat rate.
Explain that the issue was due to an incorrect contact overage fee charge despite active contacts being below the limit.
Confirm that a one-time $100 credit is being processed to resolve the discrepancy.
State clearly that the credit processing will take approximately 3 to 5 business days to reflect on the account.

incoming_email:
Subject: Critical: Experiencing HTTP 429 Rate Limit Errors on /v1/events

Hello Hiver Dev Support,

We are facing a major issue with our production integration. Our event-tracking service has started receiving HTTP 429 Too Many Requests errors when making POST requests to your `/v1/events` endpoint. This is causing data loss on our end as events are failing to sync. Our understanding was that our integration tier supported our standard volume, but our logs show that requests are being blocked. Can you check if our limits have been lowered, or if there is a rate limit adjustment we can apply? We need to get this sorted out immediately as it is impacting our live user dashboard sync.

Thanks,
Marcus Chen
Lead Integration Engineer, DataFlow Ltd.
historical_response:
Subject: Re: Critical: Experiencing HTTP 429 Rate Limit Errors on /v1/events

Dear Marcus,

Thank you for contacting Hiver Developer Support. I understand the urgency of resolving these HTTP 429 rate limit errors, as they are impacting your production data sync.

After auditing your traffic logs for the `/v1/events` endpoint, our team identified a major traffic spike on your side. Your requests reached 1,200 Requests Per Minute (RPM), which exceeds the standard Growth tier limit of 600 RPM configured for your account. This is why our API gateway began rate-limiting your requests.

To help you mitigate this issue and prevent data loss immediately, we have taken two actions:
1. We have temporarily bumped your API rate limit on the `/v1/events` endpoint to 800 RPM for the next 24 hours. This should give your team a temporary buffer to manage the current load.
2. To handle traffic spikes without data loss in the long run, we highly recommend implementing client-side exponential backoff and retry logic in your integration. This ensures that any rate-limited requests are gracefully retried rather than dropped.

If the traffic volume continues to stay at this level, we should discuss upgrading your account to the Enterprise integration tier, which supports higher baseline limits. Please let me know if the temporary bump has resolved the errors, or if you need any assistance with configuring your retry logic.

Best regards,
David K.
API Support Engineer, Hiver
ground_truth_resolution_points: Explain that the HTTP 429 errors are occurring because requests spiked to 1,200 RPM, exceeding the standard rate limit of 600 RPM on the /v1/events endpoint.
Inform the customer that their rate limit has been temporarily bumped to 800 RPM for the next 24 hours to mitigate the current issue.
Advise the customer to implement client-side exponential backoff and retry mechanisms to gracefully handle rate limit exceptions.
Suggest discussing a tier upgrade for a permanent limit increase if their baseline traffic remains above the standard limit.
